ddls:
CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the job */,
  "requested_number_of_processors" INTEGER /* Number of processors requested by the job */,
  "execution_time" INTEGER /* Estimated execution time of the job */,
  "starting_time" INTEGER /* Starting time of the job */,
  "job_priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the job (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "job_type" TEXT /* Type of the job (e.g., serial, parallel) */,
  "submission_time" INTEGER /* Time when the job was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the job (e.g., pending, running, completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jobs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processors" (
  "processor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each processor */,
  "processor_name" TEXT /* Name of the processor */,
  "processor_type" TEXT /* Type of the processor (e.g., CPU, GPU) */,
  "clock_speed" REAL /* Clock speed of the processor */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the processor (e.g., available, allocated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("processor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_processors" (
  "job_processor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job-processor allocation */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being allocated processors */,
  "processor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the processor being allocated */,
  "allocation_time" INTEGER /* Time when the processor was allocated */,
  "deallocation_time" INTEGER /* Time when the processor was deallocated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_processor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_processors_processor_id FOREIGN KEY ("processor_id") REFERENCES processors ("processor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_processors_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_execution" (
  "job_execution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job execution */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being executed */,
  "start_time" INTEGER /* Start time of the job execution */,
  "end_time" INTEGER /* End time of the job execution */,
  "execution_status" TEXT /* Status of the job execution (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "exit_code" INTEGER /* Exit code of the job execution */,
  "execution_time" INTEGER /* Actual execution time of the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_execution_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_dependencies" (
  "job_dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job dependency */,
  "dependent_job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job that depends on another job */,
  "independent_job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job that is depended on */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_dependencies_dependent_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_dependencies_independent_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("independent_job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_resources" (
  "job_resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job resource */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job that used the resource */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource (e.g., memory, disk space) */,
  "resource_usage" REAL /* Amount of the resource used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_resources_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)
